Scenario.title: Operation Triangulation Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex This scenario depicts a multi-stage cyber attack targeting the Transportation Systems sector, leveraging reconnaissance, resource development, and phishing to establish persistent access and achieve significant impact.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Potential espionage or disruption aimed at critical infrastructure.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: DNS Record, Firmware, OS Process, Network Device, Web Application, Database Server, Endpoint System, User Account
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Domain Fronting, Firmware Modification, Memory Injection, Social Engineering, Malicious File Execution, Command-Line Interface, Remote Services, Data Transfer Protocol

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC-481: Contradictory Destinations in Traffic Routing Schemes
stage.description: The attacker uses domain fronting techniques to mask their true intent while gathering information on the target organization's network infrastructure. By manipulating DNS requests and responses through contradictory routing schemes, they collect publicly available data without detection.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-682: Exploitation of Firmware or ROM Code with Unpatchable Vulnerabilities
stage.description: The threat actors develop custom firmware modification tools designed to exploit unpatchable vulnerabilities in embedded systems commonly found in transportation control equipment. These tools allow for stealthy alteration of device functionality.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers initiate contact via targeted phishing campaigns delivered through internet-facing assets, specifically employing mobile phishing tactics to deceive employees into revealing credentials or clicking malicious links related to operational technology interfaces.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, the compromised credentials or lured endpoint allows the execution of a malicious script designed to probe and interact with internal systems, potentially automating exploitation steps against identified vulnerabilities in the transportation network.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: To maintain long-term access despite potential defenses, the attackers implement fileless persistence mechanisms by leveraging remote service connections on network devices, embedding malware in legitimate processes without leaving traditional files behind.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Exploiting weaknesses gained during reconnaissance or execution phases, the threat actors escalate privileges by assigning elevated user rights through manipulated database interactions, enabling unrestricted control over critical transportation management software components.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: In order to avoid detection by security monitoring tools, the attackers employ process impersonation techniques via the command-line interface, mimicking legitimate administrative processes to hide their malicious activities within the operating system environment.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: With established access, the attackers perform extensive collection by querying system information through vulnerable web applications, identifying sensitive data such as schedules, maintenance logs, and real-time operational parameters relevant to the transportation systems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Leveraging compromised endpoints and possibly stolen credentials, the attackers move laterally across the network using abused remote desktop protocols, injecting malicious code into memory to spread undetected to connected servers managing traffic signals or dispatch systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Utilizing encrypted channels disguised as normal data transfers, the attackers exfiltrate collected sensitive information from the endpoint systems, encoding it to bypass network monitoring and evade detection focused on anomalous outbound traffic patterns.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The ultimate objective is achieved through coordinated actions that disrupt essential transportation operations, likely by triggering denial-of-service conditions or altering system configurations imperatively to cause widespread service interruptions or safety hazards.

Scenario.summary: Operation Triangulation represents a sophisticated yet accessible attack chain targeting Transportation Systems, beginning with reconnaissance and culminating in high-impact disruptions through persistence and privilege escalation.
